message:
fix(mvcc): respect DRO gate when deciding active-pivot abort in SSI T3 rule

Previously, the SSI T3 dangerous-structure detection in both
`finalize_prepared_concurrent_commit_with_ssi` and `ssi_validate_and_publish`
would unconditionally mark an active pivot transaction for abort whenever both
`has_in_rw` and `has_out_rw` flags were set. The DRO (Distributionally Robust
Optimization) decision was computed but only used for logging.

Now the code evaluates `dro_t3_decision.is_none_or(|d| d.should_abort())` once
before the edge-scan loops, and only marks the pivot for abort if the DRO gate
indicates the contention level warrants it. Under low contention (few active
readers/writers), the DRO penalty stays below threshold and the active pivot is
allowed to continue — deferring the abort to the pivot's own commit-time scan,
where it will still be caught if the dangerous structure persists.

This reduces unnecessary false-positive aborts in low-contention workloads while
preserving serializability: the pivot transaction will still be rejected at its
own commit time when it detects the full incoming+outgoing edge pair.

Test updates:
- Rename `test_ssi_marked_for_abort_via_real_edge_detection` to
  `test_ssi_low_contention_dro_defers_marked_for_abort` and update assertions
  to expect the pivot remains unmarked during T2's commit, only failing at its
  own commit.
- Rename `test_t3_rule_active_pivot_marked` to
  `test_t3_rule_active_pivot_low_contention_not_marked` with updated assertions.
- Add `test_t3_rule_active_pivot_marked_under_high_skew` that creates 20
  concurrent readers to push the DRO penalty above threshold, verifying the
  abort mark fires correctly under genuine contention.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
